data.title: Occidental Petroleum Stock Rises Amid Middle East Conflict Concerns
data.description:
Occidental Petroleum Corp (NYSE:OXY) shares are trading higher on Friday amid growing concerns regarding the ongoing U.S.–Israel–Iran conflict now stretching into its seventh day, along with intensified after reports that an Iranian strike hit an oil tanker near Kuwait.

  • Occidental Petroleum stock is trading at elevated levels. What should traders watch with OXY?

Tanker Incident Heightens Regional Risk

According to the United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations (UKMTO) agency, the tanker's captain reported a loud explosion on the port side before spotting a small craft speeding away. The vessel began taking on water and an oil spill was observed, though there were no fires and the crew remained safe.

The incident adds to the already‑tense situation in the region, where Iran has shut down the Strait of Hormuz and warned it will target any vessel attempting to pass through. The closure has pushed global oil prices higher.

Strait of Hormuz Closure Fuels Supply Concerns

The Strait of Hormuz has roughly 20% of the world's oil supply moving through its waters. Iran's control over the strait gives it enormous leverage over global oil shipments, and keeping it closed for an extended period is already causing serious disruptions to supply.

The U.S. Treasury Department underscored the seriousness of the situation by granting India a temporary waiver to continue buying Russian oil, a move aimed at keeping global supplies from tightening even further. India relies heavily on imported crude, much of it from the Middle East, making the strait's closure especially disruptive.

Meanwhile, the conflict itself shows no signs of easing. Israel's military leadership has said the joint U.S.–Israel campaign is entering a new phase aimed at further weakening Iran's military capabilities.

Technical Outlook: Momentum Remains Strong

Occidental Petroleum is currently trading above its key moving averages, indicating strong bullish momentum. The stock is positioned well above its 20-day, 50-day and 100-day SMAs, which suggests that traders might look for continued upward movement in the near term.

The RSI is currently at 68.35, which is in neutral territory but approaching overbought levels, suggesting that while there is still momentum, traders should be cautious as the stock nears a potential pullback if it crosses into overbought territory.

MACD is above its signal line, indicating bullish momentum for Occidental’s stock. This positive signal reinforces the strength of the current trend, suggesting that traders might consider buying on dips.

Key support is at $43.50, and if this level is tested, it could provide a buying opportunity if the stock holds above it. Without a clear resistance level above, traders should monitor for potential breakout points as the stock approaches its recent highs.

The golden cross in February, when the 50-day SMA crossed above the 200-day SMA, further supports the bullish trend. This crossover is a strong indicator of a potential long-term uptrend, making it an important signal for traders to watch.

Over the past 12 months, Occidental’s stock has gained 18.03%, reflecting a solid longer-term uptrend. This performance indicates that the stock has been resilient and could continue to attract buyers if the momentum persists.

Currently, the stock is positioned at 88.6% of its 52-week range, nearing its highs. This positioning suggests that traders should be vigilant for signs of a breakout or a reversal as it approaches these critical levels.

OXY Price Action: Occidental shares were up 0.68% at $53.60 at the time of publication on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro.

Image: Piotr Swat/Shutterstock

data.title: These Analysts Increase Their Forecasts On Ciena Following Upbeat Q1 Earnings
data.description:
Ciena Corp (NYSE:CIEN) posted upbeat earnings for its first quarter on Thursday.

The company posted adjusted earnings of $1.35 per share, beating market estimates of $1.17 per share. The company's sales came in at $1.427 billion versus estimates of $1.399 billion.

Ciena raised its FY2026 sales guidance from $5.700 billion-$6.100 billion to $5.900 billion-$6.300 billion.

Ciena shares gained 2.8% to trade at $307.39 on Friday.

These analysts made changes to their price targets on Ciena following earnings announcement.

data.title: Liquidia Analysts Boost Their Forecasts After Q4 Earnings
data.description:
Liquidia Corp (NASDAQ:LQDA) reported mixed results for the fourth quarter on Thursday.

The company posted quarterly earnings of 15 cents per share which missed the analyst consensus estimate of 17 cents per share. The company reported quarterly sales of $92.021 million which beat the analyst consensus estimate of $83.634 million.

Dr. Roger Jeffs, Liquidia’s Chief Executive Officer, said, “As we close 2025, we are encouraged by how quickly YUTREPIA® has taken hold in clinical practice, placing it among one of the top specialty drug launches over the past five years across all therapeutic categories. Adoption continues to broaden across PAH and PH-ILD, with increasing depth in treatment centers and more experience transitioning appropriate patients from oral, inhaled and systemic prostacyclin therapies.”

Liquidia shares gained 4.5% to trade at $36.08 on Friday.

These analysts made changes to their price targets on Guidewire Software following earnings announcement.

  • HC Wainwright & Co. analyst Andrew S. Fein maintained Liquidia with a Buy and raised the price target from $50 to $55.
  • Wells Fargo analyst Benjamin Burnett maintained the stock with an Overweight rating and raised the price target from $31 to $44.

Considering buying LQDA stock? Here’s what analysts think:

Photo via Shutterstock

data.title: Here's How Much You Would Have Made Owning iShares Gold Trust Shares Stock In The Last 10 Years
data.description:
iShares Gold Trust Shares (NYSE:IAU) has outperformed the market over the past 10 years by 1.75% on an annualized basis producing an average annual return of 14.71%. Currently, iShares Gold Trust Shares has a market capitalization of $82.53 billion.

Buying $100 In IAU: If an investor had bought $100 of IAU stock 10 years ago, it would be worth $400.75 today based on a price of $96.79 for IAU at the time of writing.

data.title: Elon Says Diet Coke Is 'Awesome' — 'I Don't Care If Drinking Gallons Of It Shaves A Bit Of Life Off. Worth It'
data.description:
Elon Musk will debate artificial intelligence, electric vehicles and space travel. Diet Coke is not one of the debates.

In 2023 Elon Musk reacted on X to a post highlighting Warren Buffett's concentrated holdings inside Berkshire Hathaway, including a sizable stake in the Coca-Cola Company. As users discussed Buffett's allocation to Coke, Musk chimed in directly in the thread.

Then he made it personal.

"Diet Coke & Coke Zero are awesome," he wrote. "I don't care if drinking gallons of it shaves a bit of life off. Worth it."

The remark lived inside that X conversation. It was not a speech. It was not an interview. It was Musk typing in real time while reacting to Buffett's Coca-Cola exposure.

Buffett's connection to Coke is financial and habitual. Berkshire has owned roughly 400 million Coca-Cola shares since 1988, collecting hundreds of millions annually in dividends. 

Trending: A single bad hire can set a startup back years. Here are the 5 hires founders most often misjudge — and why

Musk's version of that loyalty looks different. He drinks the zero-sugar line and openly acknowledges the possible trade-off.

The Health Question Behind The Post

Artificial sweeteners such as aspartame and sucralose have faced renewed scrutiny. A 2022 study published in the British Medical Journal linked higher artificial sweetener intake to a 9% increased risk of cardiovascular disease. Research cited by the American Heart Association associated daily diet soda consumption with increased risks of ischemic stroke and dementia in older adults.

Regular sugary soda carries established links to obesity and Type 2 diabetes when consumed heavily.

Musk did not dispute any of that in the thread. He reduced the issue to preference.

Coke As Dividend And Daily Habit

Coca-Cola remains one of Berkshire Hathaway's most recognizable holdings and a long-term dividend engine.

At the same time, the zero-sugar versions Musk drinks have become central to Coca-Cola's modern growth strategy as consumers shift away from full-sugar soda.

One billionaire profits from Coke through ownership. Both drink it. 

Image: Shutterstock
